title: Auto Review Pipeline Runner Receipt
description: Deterministic provenance record written by the runner for each automatic adapter invocation and routing decision. It records WHAT was invoked and the resulting budget/state change, never the sensitive HOW. Forbidden content: the receipt MUST NOT contain an expanded adapter command, expanded shell alias, environment dump, token, cookie, secret, or any model-selected next action. Adapter invocation is recorded only as an adapter id plus a registry command reference (agents/registry.yaml path), never the expanded command line or its environment. 'next_transition' is constrained to the frozen workflow transition set so a model cannot inject an arbitrary next hop; 'failure_class' is constrained to the frozen failure-class set. Raw model output, prompts, and verdict bytes are referenced by path and persisted unaltered elsewhere; the receipt never embeds them. If provider output itself exposes a secret, the runner stops and escalates rather than rewriting evidence here.

safe_repo_relative_path.description: Nullable repo-relative evidence path. null when not applicable for this node; a non-null value must be a non-empty repo-relative path and is rejected if it is absolute, contains parent-directory traversal ('..'), or contains CR, LF, tab, or any other control character.

sequence.description: Monotonic per-run sequence number for this receipt.

node.description: Runner workflow node that produced this receipt. Only model-adapter nodes emit receipts; deterministic seal/commit steps are recorded by the seal receipt and status, not here.

attempt.description: Attempt index within this node (e.g. invalid-JSON retry raises this). Every attempt that begins adapter execution consumes one call from the budget.

review_unit_id.description: Review unit id this call belongs to, when applicable (v1 review units are task-only). Always present; null only when the node has no unit (e.g. an implementation call before any unit is sealed).

task_id.description: Task id this call belongs to, when applicable. Always present; null only when the call is not task-scoped.

adapter.description: Adapter reference. Records only the adapter id and the registry path of the command template used; never the expanded command, shell alias, arguments, or environment. The registry_command_ref must be one of the frozen anchored references below and must match both the adapter id and a command key that the workflow allows for this node.

id.description: Adapter id from agents/registry.yaml that the runner invoked.

registry_command_ref.description: Anchored registry reference to the exact command template used. Only these frozen references are valid; the expanded command, availability probes, yolo commands, same-prefix guesses, shell fragments, whitespace/newline strings, and secret-like arbitrary values are all schema-invalid. The expanded command is never stored.

allOf.description: adapter.id and the <id> segment of registry_command_ref must match.

prompt_path.description: Path to the immutable prompt body fed to the adapter, when applicable. Safe repo-relative path; null when the node has no prompt file.

raw_output_path.description: Path to the captured raw adapter output, persisted unaltered, when applicable. Safe repo-relative path; null when no raw output was captured.

verdict_path.description: Path to the accepted schema-valid verdict JSON (final-and-only block), when applicable. Safe repo-relative path; null when the node produces no verdict.

started_at.description: ISO8601 instant when adapter execution began. The call is charged to the budget at this point.

completed_at.description: ISO8601 instant when adapter execution completed, or null if it did not complete cleanly.

exit_status.description: Adapter process exit status, or null if not applicable.

timeout.description: True if the adapter process exceeded its adapter-specific timeout (the registered per-adapter timeout_seconds, or a command-specific override such as grok.optional_review_timeout_seconds, from agents/registry.yaml). A timeout still consumes one call.

call_budget.description: Model-call budget immediately before and after this attempt. 'after' MUST equal 'before - 1' once adapter execution has begun, regardless of success, timeout, provider failure, invalid JSON, or empty output.

failure_class.description: Sanitized failure class from the frozen failure-class set, or null on success. Never contains command text, environment, or secrets.

next_transition.description: Fixed workflow transition the runner will apply next, selected only from the committed workflow allowed_next_transitions set (see workflows/templates/stage-delivery.yaml auto_review_pipeline.executable_contract.allowed_next_transitions). The set excludes any manual-only hop such as a bookkeeper decision. A model cannot select this value; it is never an arbitrary model-supplied next hop. null means the runner stops without applying a further transition.

allOf.description: implementation/fix nodes use the adapter's existing noninteractive/write command reference.
embedded_cross_check nodes use the adapter's existing read-only/embedded command reference.

review_1 nodes use exactly one of the three frozen review-1 routes: Grok primary (optional_review_command), or a Kimi/Claude-GLM serial-unit fallback (each provider's embedded_read_only_review_command). Schema validity alone never declares fallback eligibility; serial-only author-set eligibility and the exhausted-route escalation remain runner/validator checks.
